privileged_info_summary: The esophageal cancer dataset used in this task is the real Breslow-Day esoph dataset from a case-control study conducted in Ille-et-Vilaine, France. It contains 200 esophageal cancer cases and 975 controls stratified by age, alcohol consumption, and tobacco consumption across 88 strata. Chi-square tests for linear trend (age-adjusted) show: Alcohol trend chi-square = 134.87 (p < 2.2e-16), well within the expected range of 50-200; Tobacco trend chi-square = 32.859 (p = 9.908e-09), well within the expected range of 15-100. Both alcohol and tobacco show significant dose-response relationships with esophageal cancer risk. The dataset has been widely used in epidemiological research and statistics education since its publication in Breslow and Day's 1980 textbook.
